revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:阿智神社 (阿智村)]] {{Expand Japanese|阿智神社 (阿智村)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Achi Shrine (Q11657447) | native_name = 阿智神社 | image = Achi-jinja, maemiya haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Achi Shrine (Q11657447) | coordinates = {{coord|35.457344|137.716495}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Omoikane|lt=Omoikane|en|Omoikane|fr|Omoikane|ja|オモイカネ|ka|ომოიკანე|ko|오모이카네|mni|ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)|ru|Омойканэ|sv|Omoigane|zh|思兼神|qid=Q586088|1=Omoikane}} }} {{nihongo|'''Achi Shrine'''|阿智神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shinano Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11386164|ltq=Q11386164|qid=Q11386164|1=List of Shikinaisha in Shinano Province}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P155: {{ill|Oyamada Shrine|lt=Oyamada Shrine|ja|大山田神社|qid=Q85878781|1=Oyamada Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q218813|ltq=Q218813|qid=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Achi, Nagano|lt=Achi|ceb|Achi-mura|en|Achi, Nagano|envoyage|Achi|es|Achi (Nagano)|fa|اچی، ناگانو|fi|Achi|fr|Achi (Nagano)|hy|Ատի (գյուղ)|it|Achi (Giappone)|ja|阿智村|ko|아치촌|nl|Achi (Nagano)|pl|Achi (Nagano)|ru|Ати (село)|tl|Achi, Nagano|tr|Achi, Japonya|uk|Аті (Наґано)|uz|Achi (Nagano)|zh|阿智村|qid=Q1203279|1=Achi, Nagano}} * {{ill|Shinano Province|lt=Shinano Province|ckb|پارێزگای شینانۆ|cs|Provincie Šinano|de|Provinz Shinano|en|Shinano Province|eo|Provinco Ŝinano|es|Provincia de Shinano|fa|ولایت شینانو|fr|Province de Shinano|gl|Provincia de Shinano|hak|Shinano-koet|id|Provinsi Shinano|it|Shinano (provincia)|ja|信濃国|ko|시나노국|lt|Šinano žemė|ms|Wilayah Shinano|nl|Shinano (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Shinano|pt|Província de Shinano|ru|Синано (провинция)|simple|Shinano Province|sk|Šinano (provincia)|sr|Провинција Шинано|th|แคว้นชินาโนะ|tr|Shinano (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сінано|zh_min_nan|Sinano-kok|zh_yue|信濃國|zh|信濃國|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} * {{ill|Q11380911|ltq=Q11380911|qid=Q11380911|1=Ina district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.457344|137.716495}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Achi-jinja, maemiya haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Omoikane|lt=Omoikane|en|Omoikane|fr|Omoikane|ja|オモイカネ|ka|ომოიკანე|ko|오모이카네|mni|ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)|ru|Омойканэ|sv|Omoigane|zh|思兼神|qid=Q586088|1=Omoikane}} * {{ill|Q10940723|ltq=Q10940723|qid=Q10940723|1=Uwaharu and Shitaharu}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181970 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 7272125 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Achino Shrine|lt=Achino Shrine|qid=Q135039913|1=Achino Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奥宮：長野県下伊那郡阿智村智里497 前宮：長野県下伊那郡阿智村智里489', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長野県下伊那郡阿智村智里', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:阿智神社 (阿智村)]] {{wikidata link|Q11657447}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Achi Shrine |Image = [[File:Achi-jinja, maemiya haiden.jpg|270px]]
Maemiya Worship Hall |Location = Okumiya: {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shimoina District|Q726999|qid=Q726999|1=Shimoina District, Nagano}}{{ill|lt=Achi Village|Q1203279|qid=Q1203279|1=Achi, Nagano}} Chisato 497
Maemiya: Chisato 489, Achi Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture |Coordinates = Okumiya: {{WikiCoordinates2|35|27|49.9|N|137|42|14.5|E|display=inline}}
Maemiya: {{WikiCoordinates2|35|27|26.5|N|137|42|59.4|E|display=inline,title}} | Label position = left |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Uwaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map+|Japan Nagano|width=210|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=26.5|lon_deg=137|lon_min=42|lon_sec=59.4|position=right|label=Maemiya|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=35|lat_min=27|lat_sec=49.9|lon_deg=137|lon_min=42|lon_sec=14.5|position=left|label=Okumiya|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35.448886|N|137.720521|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=10|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|27|26.5|N|137|42|59.4|E}}|label1=Maemiya|label-pos1=right|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|27|49.9|N|137|42|14.5|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=left|mark-size2=8}} }} [[File:Sacred rock at Okumiya.jpg|Thumbnail|220px|right|Sacred rock at the Okumiya]] [[File:Approach to Achi Shrine Okumiya.jpg|Thumbnail|220px|right|Approach to Achi Shrine Okumiya]] '''Achi Shrine''' (あちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Achi Village|Q1203279|qid=Q1203279|1=Achi, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Shimoina District|Q726999|qid=Q726999|1=Shimoina District, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|qid=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},” {{ill|lt=Omoikane|Q586088|qid=Q586088|lt=Ame-no-Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto|1=Omoikane}} descended from heaven and became the ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Achi clan|Q109594403|qid=Q109594403|lt=Shinano no Achi Hafuribe|1=Achi clan}}, making this an ancient shrine with a venerable history. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Uwaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} : ; Associated deities :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} ※ There are a Maemiya and an Okumiya; the Okumiya contains a {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} (sacred rock). === History === According to shrine tradition, in the spring of the first month of the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|qid=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}, {{ill|lt=Omoikane|Q586088|qid=Q586088|lt=Ame-no-Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto|1=Omoikane}} descended to Shinano Province accompanied by his divine children and became the ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Achi clan|Q109594403|qid=Q109594403|lt=Achi no Hafuribe|1=Achi clan}}. The rituals were thereafter carried out by the Hara clan, his descendants. This area lay along the route of the ancient {{ill|lt=Tōsandō|Q1334011|qid=Q1334011|1=Tōsandō}}. The place name Hirugami, the site of the shrine, is said to originate from a legend in which {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258}} was returning from his eastern campaign and attempted to cross {{ill|lt=Kamisaka Pass|Q11588847|qid=Q11588847|1=Misaka Pass}}, but was blocked by the poisonous miasma of violent deities dwelling there. When he happened to spit out the garlic he was chewing, the evil gods immediately fell, allowing him to proceed. Because of this, the area was called Hirugami (“garlic-chewing”), which later became Hirugami (“day god”). Another theory holds that Omoikane, who descended to this land, was the foremost god of wisdom in Takamagahara, devising plans for all affairs and bringing them to fruition. In particular, when {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, angered by the outrageous behavior of her brother {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, hid herself in the {{ill|lt=Amano-Iwato|Q3231835|qid=Q3231835|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|1=Amano-Iwato}}, it was through Omoikane’s direction that the sun goddess Amaterasu was safely brought back outside. Because Omoikane rendered such great service—returning the world from darkness to daylight—the place where he settled is said to be called Hirugami (“day god”). The Okumiya lies about 2 kilometers upstream from the Maemiya along the {{ill|lt=Achi River|Q110454496|qid=Q110454496|1=Achi River}}, at the triangular, peninsula-like point where the Motodani River and the Kurokawa River merge to form the Achi River, a place known as Kawaigaryō. On the hilltop stands a moss-covered giant rock that is venerated. {{ill|lt=Iwao Ōba|Q11433904|qid=Q11433904|1=Iwao Ōba}}, a professor at {{ill|lt=Kokugakuin University|Q495330|qid=Q495330|1=Kokugakuin University}}, demonstrated that this rock is an {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}}, that is, a relic of ancient {{ill|lt=Shinto rituals|Q109937652|qid=Q109937652|lt=ritual practices|1=Shinto ritual}}{{Cite book|Japanese|title=Achi Village History, Volume 1|date=1984-03-20|year=1984|publisher=Achi Village History Publication Committee}}. Although it is a naturally formed giant stone close to a diamond shape, its protrusions are said to align with the four cardinal directions. It is also said that at the time of the winter solstice, the sun rises along its eastern extension. It is said that during the Tenryaku era under the reign of Emperor Murakami, the Tokutake clan enshrined as branch deities at the Nakasha (enshrined deity: Ame-no-Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto) and Hōkōsha (enshrined deity: Ame-no-Uwaharu-no-Mikoto) of {{ill|lt=Togakushi Shrine|Q218828|qid=Q218828|1=Togakushi Shrine}}, formerly a national shrine located in Togakushi Village, Kamiminochi District{{Cite book|Japanese|author=|title=Shimoina History, Vol. 4, Shimoina History Compilation Committee, Representative Ichimura Kaneto|date=|year=1961|publisher=Shinano Education Association Publishing Department}}. For a time, it was also called {{ill|lt=Sannō Gongen|Q11470054|qid=Q11470054|lt=Sannō Gongen Shrine|1=Sannō Gongen}}, as confirmed by {{ill|lt=shuinjō|Q11093673|qid=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} (vermillion seal documents). In addition, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amekaharu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=kunizukuri|Q2483673|qid=Q2483673|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} and an enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Chichibu Shrine|Q2963366|qid=Q2963366|1=Chichibu Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, which was worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Chichibu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q5096034|qid=Q5096034|1=Chichibu Province}}, is said to be the younger brother of Ame-no-Uwaharu-no-Mikoto. According to the “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saibe-shi Kaden|1=UNKNOWN}},” {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|Q849412|qid=Q849412|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} is said to be a distant ancestor of the Achi Hafuri. === Local information === ; Address * Maemiya – Chisato 489, Achi Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture * Okumiya – Chisato 497, Achi Village, Shimoina District, Nagano Prefecture ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Sonohara Interchange|Q10927469|qid=Q10927469|1=Sonohara interchange}}. ; Nearby * {{ill|lt=Hirugami Onsen|Q11514242|qid=Q11514242|1=Hirugami Onsen}} * {{ill|lt=Sonohara|Q60997587|qid=Q60997587|1=Sonohara}} === References === * “Chisato Village History,” edited by the Chisato Village Youth Association, Representative Ishihara Mamoru, published by the Chisato Village Youth Association, 1934 (Shōwa 9) * “Shimoina History, Vol. 4,” Shimoina History Compilation Committee, Representative {{ill|lt=Ichimura Kaneto|Q56253217|qid=Q56253217|1=Minato Ichimura}}, published by Shinano Education Association Publishing Department, 1961 (Shōwa 36) * “Place Names of Nagano Prefecture,” Japanese Historical Place Names Series 20, supervised by {{ill|lt=Isshi Shigeki|Q20038730|qid=Q20038730|1=Shigeki Isshi}}, published by Heibonsha, 1975 (Shōwa 50) * “Achi Village History,” edited by the Achi Village History Editorial Committee, published by the Achi Village History Publication Committee, 1984 (Shōwa 59) * “{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}},” Volume 1, Age of the Gods, Section 7 main text, Ama-no-Iwato * “{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}},” Upper Volume, 3-4 Ama-no-Iwayado * “{{ill|lt=Hotsuma Tsutae|Q2083538|qid=Q2083538|lt=Hotsuma Tsutae|1=Hotsuma Tsutae}},” Aya 1 Kitsuno Nato Homushi Saru; Aya 7 Nokoshifumi Saka o Tatsu; Aya 10 Kashima Tachi Tsuri Tai * “{{ill|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|Q2716751|qid=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}},” Volume 2 Jingi Hongi; Volume 3 Tenjin Hongi === Footnotes === === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Ōmikami Shrine|Q11435435|qid=Q11435435|1=Omike Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Jishu Shrine|Q10928238|qid=Q10928238|1=Jishu Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Abuchi Shrine|Q65260948|qid=Q65260948|1=Afuchi Shrine}} – Located in the same village, Komaba, and likewise enshrines Ame-no-Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto. Abuchi Shrine also enshrines {{ill|lt=Silla|Q28456|qid=Q28456|1=Silla}} and {{ill|lt=Myōjin|Q5369590|qid=Q5369590|1=Myōjin}}. === External links === * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/03hani/6093.htm Achi Shrine – Nagano Prefecture Shrine Association] {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto navigation}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Achi Shrine (Achi Village)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Achi Shrine (Achi Village)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Achi Shrine (Achi Village)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:阿智村の建築物]] [[Category:阿智村の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]
